2026-04-15T22:33:06.0045069Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-15T22:33:06.0045534Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-15T22:33:06.0045918Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-15T22:33:06.0046220Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-15T22:33:06.0046451Z [36;1m[0m

This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

2026-04-15T22:36:15.0115994Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-15T22:34:11.387950954Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58189,\"title\":\"[Communication] Docs: Update README to document Unified Messaging APIs (ConversationAdministrationClient, ConversationThreadClie\\n[Content truncated due to length]\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Communication.Messages`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/`\\n**Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (#56911) by `@MaiLinhP`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #56911 migrated `Azure.Communication.Messages` to the new TypeSpec emitter. The migration brought in (or confirmed the presence of) two new hand-authored clients — `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient` — as part of the Unified Messaging feature set first introduced in 1.3.0-beta.1. Custom suppression of `BuilderExtension` and `ClientSettings` was also added for types using `CommunicationTokenCredential` to preserve AOT compatibility.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `README.md` covers only the legacy WhatsApp-specific notification path (`NotificationMessagesClient`, `MessageTemplateClient`). The new **Unified Messaging** surface — `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient` — is completely undocumented.\\n- No examples show how to authenticate or instantiate `ConversationAdministrationClient` or `ConversationThreadClient`, including via `CommunicationTokenCredential` (whose `BuilderExtension`/`ClientSettings` were explicitly customised in this PR).\\n- `ConversationThreadClientSettings` (new custom type added in this PR) is not mentioned anywhere in user-facing docs.\\n- The `## Key concepts` section is a collapsed sub-heading inside `## Getting started` rather than a standalone `## Key concepts` H2 section, and its content is a single line that does not describe the two new clients.\\n- The CHANGELOG `## 1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` section is empty; the generator migration and new client-settings type should be acknowledged there.\\n- No sample test file (`tests/Samples/`) exists for the Unified Messaging APIs (conversations), so there are no snippet sources for the README.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### 1a. Update `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nAdd entries under the `## 1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` header:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.3.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n- Migrated library to new TypeSpec-based emitter.\\n- Added `ConversationThreadClientSettings` to support configuring `ConversationThreadClient` with `CommunicationTokenCredential`.\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Update `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md`\\n\\nRestructure the README to comply with the standard template. Key changes:\\n\\n1. Move `### Key concepts` out of `## Getting started` and make it a top-level `## Key concepts` H2 section.\\n2. Expand **Key concepts** to describe all four clients: `NotificationMessagesClient`, `MessageTemplateClient`, `ConversationAdministrationClient`, `ConversationThreadClient`.\\n3. Add a new **Authenticate with CommunicationTokenCredential** sub-section showing how to create a `ConversationThreadClient` with a token credential.\\n4. Add new **Examples** sub-sections for Unified Messaging: creating a conversation, listing conversations, sending/receiving messages on a conversation thread.\\n\\nSuggested **Key concepts** section content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n- `NotificationMessagesClient` — sends outbound notification messages (WhatsApp, SMS).\\n- `MessageTemplateClient` — retrieves approved message templates.\\n- `ConversationAdministrationClient` — creates and manages conversation threads (Unified Messaging).\\n- `ConversationThreadClient` — sends and receives messages within a conversation thread (Unified Messaging).\\n```\\n\\nSuggested **Authenticate the client** addition (append after the existing connection-string example):\\n\\n```markdown\\n#### CommunicationTokenCredential\\n\\n`ConversationThreadClient` also supports authentication via `CommunicationTokenCredential`:\\n\\n```\\nvar endpoint = new Uri(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\nvar tokenCredential = new CommunicationTokenCredential(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\nvar settings = new ConversationThreadClientSettings(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, endpoint, tokenCredential);\\nConversationThreadClient conversationThreadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(settings);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new sample test file at:\\n`sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/tests/Samples/Sample_ConversationMessaging.cs`\\n\\nThe file should:\\n- Include `[Test]` (NUnit) methods, one per snippet.\\n- Wrap snippet code with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`.\\n- Wrap test-only teardown/assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`.\\n\\nSnippets to implement:\\n\\n**Snippet: `CreateConversationAdministrationClient`**\\n```csharp\\n#region Snippet:CreateConversationAdministrationClient\\nvar connectionString = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\nConversationAdministrationClient adminClient = new ConversationAdministrationClient(connectionString);\\n#endregion\\n```\\n\\n**Snippet: `CreateConversationThreadClientWithToken`** (referenced in README above)\\n```csharp\\n#region Snippet:CreateConversationThreadClientWithToken\\nvar endpoint = new Uri(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\nvar tokenCredential = new CommunicationTokenCredential(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\nvar settings = new ConversationThreadClientSettings(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, endpoint, tokenCredential);\\nConversationThreadClient conversationThreadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(settings);\\n#endregion\\n```\\n\\n**Snippet: `ListConversations`**\\n```csharp\\n#region Snippet:ListConversations\\nAsyncPageable conversations = adminClient.GetConversationsAsync();\\nawait foreach (ConversationItem conversation in conversations)\\n{\\n    Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Conversation id: {conversation.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n}\\n#endregion\\n```\\n\\nAdd README placeholders at the appropriate locations:\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` must contain these sections as top-level H2 (`##`) headings in this order:\\n\\n1. ✅ **Getting started** — present but `Key concepts` is incorrectly nested inside it\\n2. ❌ **Key concepts** — must be moved to its own `##` section and expanded to cover all four clients\\n3. ✅ **Examples** — present but missing Unified Messaging examples\\n4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present\\n5. ✅ **Next steps** — present\\n6. ✅ **Contributing** — present\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 communication` — confirm the README is updated with the injected snippet bodies\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 communication` — regenerates API listing if any public types changed\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24481510665/agentic_workflow) · ● 337.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58189\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-15T22:30:57Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-15T22:30:57Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

Move `### Key concepts` out of `## Getting started` and make it a top-level `## Key concepts` H2 section.\\n2. Expand **Key concepts** to describe all four clients: `NotificationMessagesClient`, `MessageTemplateClient`, `ConversationAdministrationClient`, `ConversationThreadClient`.\\n3. Add a new **Authenticate with CommunicationTokenCredential** sub-section showing how to create a `ConversationThreadClient` with a token credential.\\n4. Add new **Examples** sub-sections for Unified Messaging: creating a conversation, listing conversations, sending/receiving messages on a conversation thread.\\n\\nSuggested **Key concepts** section content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n- `NotificationMessagesClient` — sends outbound notification messages (WhatsApp, SMS).\\n- `MessageTemplateClient` — retrieves approved message templates.\\n- `ConversationAdministrationClient` — creates and manages conversation threads (Unified Messaging).\\n- `ConversationThreadClient` — sends and receives messages within a conversation thread (Unified Messaging).\\n```\\n\\nSuggested **Authenticate the client** addition (append after the existing connection-string example):\\n\\n```markdown\\n#### CommunicationTokenCredential\\n\\n`ConversationThreadClient` also supports authentication via `CommunicationTokenCredential`:\\n\\n```\\nvar endpoint = new Uri(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\nvar tokenCredential = new CommunicationTokenCredential(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\nvar settings = new ConversationThreadClientSettings(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, endpoint, tokenCredential);\\nConversationThreadClient conversationThreadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(settings);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new sample test file at:\\n`sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/tests/Samples/Sample_ConversationMessaging.cs`\\n\\nThe file should:\\n- Include `[Test]` (NUnit) methods, one per snippet.\\n- Wrap snippet code with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`.\\n- Wrap test-only teardown/assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`.\\n\\nSnippets to implement:\\n\\n**Snippet: `CreateConversationAdministrationClient`**\\n```csharp\\n#region Snippet:CreateConversationAdministrationClient\\nvar connectionString = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\nConversationAdministrationClient adminClient = new ConversationAdministrationClient(connectionString);\\n#endregion\\n```\\n\\n**Snippet: `CreateConversationThreadClientWithToken`** (referenced in README above)\\n```csharp\\n#region Snippet:CreateConversationThreadClientWithToken\\nvar endpoint = new Uri(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\nvar tokenCredential = new CommunicationTokenCredential(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\nvar settings = new ConversationThreadClientSettings(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, endpoint, tokenCredential);\\nConversationThreadClient conversationThreadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(settings);\\n#endregion\\n```\\n\\n**Snippet: `ListConversations`**\\n```csharp\\n#region Snippet:ListConversations\\nAsyncPageable conversations = adminClient.GetConversationsAsync();\\nawait foreach (ConversationItem conversation in conversations)\\n{\\n    Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Conversation id: {conversation.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n}\\n#endregion\\n```\\n\\nAdd README placeholders at the appropriate locations:\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` must contain these sections as top-level H2 (`##`) headings in this order:\\n\\n1. ✅ **Getting started** — present but `Key concepts` is incorrectly nested inside it\\n2. ❌ **Key concepts** — must be moved to its own `##` section and expanded to cover all four clients\\n3. ✅ **Examples** — present but missing Unified Messaging examples\\n4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present\\n5. ✅ **Next steps** — present\\n6. ✅ **Contributing** — present\\n\\nUse
